issuing_office: Office of Associate Chief Counsel (Corporate), CC:CORP:02
uilc_dictionary_mapping:
code: 0382.00-00 | title: Limitation on Net Operating Loss Carryforwards and Certain Built-in Losses Following Ownership Changes
code: 0382.07-00 | title: Owner Shift, Equity Structure Shift and Testing Date
code: 0382.07-05 | title: Segregation Rules - Public Groups and Overlapping Public Groups
key_authorities_cited:
[Segregation Rules]
Treas. Reg. § 1.382-2T(j)(2) - segregation rules upon equity structure shift
Treas. Reg. § 1.382-2T(j)(2)(iii)(B)(1) - presumption that two public groups upon reorganization do not overlap
Treas. Reg. § 1.382-2T(k)(2) - actual knowledge of stock ownership
Treas. Reg. § 1.382-3(a) - entity rules
transaction_type:
Reverse triangular merger / tax-free reorganization (§ 368(a)(1)(A) by reason of § 368(a)(2)(E), or § 368(a)(1)(B))
Reverse acquisition within meaning of § 1.1502-75(d)(3)
Both Acquiring and Target are widely-held publicly traded consolidated groups with NOLs
issues_presented:
(1) Whether Written Inquiries to potential Overlapping Shareholders constitute an acceptable method of determining Actual Knowledge under § 1.382-2T(k)(2).
(2) Whether Acquiring may use that Actual Knowledge to treat the Overlapping Public Group as an additional direct public group for purposes of the segregation rules of § 1.382-2T(j)(2), with the presumption in § 1.382-2T(j)(2)(iii)(B)(1) not applying.
(3) Whether there is any requirement to inquire as to ownership throughout the entire testing period.
conclusions:
(1) YES - Written Inquiries are an acceptable method of determining actual knowledge within § 1.382-2T(k)(2).
(2) YES - Acquiring may treat the Overlapping Public Group as an additional direct public group; the presumption described in § 1.382-2T(j)(2)(iii)(B)(1) will not apply.
(3) NO - No requirement to inquire as to ownership throughout the entire testing period; only immediately before the Proposed Transaction.
subject_summary: Segregation-rule presumption rebuttal: Acquiring uses Written Inquiries (telephone/email follow-up after IR-department and SEC-filing review) to obtain Actual Knowledge of Overlapping Public Group ownership; IRS rules that the presumption described in § 1.382-2T(j)(2)(iii)(B)(1) will not apply, allowing the overlap to be recognized as a single direct public group rather than two presumptively non-overlapping groups.
timeline_vector: [Date 4 (pre-transaction testing date): Acquiring has only direct public groups (§ 1.382-2T(j)(2)(ii)); cumulative owner shift < c%] -> [Date 4: Target has three known 5% shareholders owning h%; cumulative owner shift ~i%] -> [Date 3: Acquiring + Target execute merger Agreement after board approvals] -> [Pre-closing window: Acquiring conducts Phase 1 (IR inquiries, electronic research, SEC filings review) to develop list of potential Overlapping Shareholders] -> [Pre-closing window: Acquiring conducts Phase 2 (direct phone/email contact + Written Inquiries) to confirm Overlapping Shareholder identity and share counts] -> [Effective time: Merger Sub merges into Target; Target survives as wholly-owned subsidiary of Acquiring; qualifies as § 368(a) reorganization and § 1.1502-75(d)(3) reverse acquisition] -> [Post-closing: former Acquiring shareholders own k% of Acquiring; former Target shareholders own l%] -> [Testing date measurement: Overlapping Public Group treated as single additional direct public group; presumption of § 1.382-2T(j)(2)(iii)(B)(1) does not apply]
timeline_vector_raw: [Date 4: Acquiring testing-date snapshot - no 5% shareholders other than direct public groups] -> [Date 4: Target testing-date snapshot - three 5% shareholders owning h%; cumulative owner shift ~i%] -> [Date 3: Agreement executed] -> [Phase 1: IR inquiries + SEC filings review] -> [Phase 2: Written Inquiry sent to potential Overlapping Shareholders] -> [Closing: § 368(a)(1)(A)/(2)(E) or (B) reorganization; § 1.1502-75(d)(3) reverse acquisition] -> [Post-closing ownership: Acquiring shareholders k%, former Target shareholders l%]
favorable_logic_pivot: The presumption of § 1.382-2T(j)(2)(iii)(B)(1) - that two public groups created on a single equity structure shift do not overlap - is a PRESUMPTION, not an immutable rule. The pivot: when a taxpayer obtains Actual Knowledge under § 1.382-2T(k)(2) of actual overlap, the regulatory presumption yields. Acquiring's two-phase methodology (research + Written Inquiry) is sufficient process to establish that actual knowledge, even though it does not extend back through the entire testing period - the inquiry is appropriately limited to ownership immediately before the transaction. Result: Overlapping Public Group is collapsed to a single direct public group instead of being counted as two presumptively distinct groups, reducing measured owner shift.
compelled_representations:
(a) Acquiring is a loss corporation as defined in § 382(k)(1).
(b) Acquiring's only class of outstanding stock during the relevant testing period is its common stock.
(c) Acquiring has no other outstanding interests or obligations treated as stock for purposes of § 382.
(d) Prior to the Proposed Transaction, Acquiring will have no actual knowledge other than (i) corporate records of Acquiring and Target, (ii) actual knowledge obtained in determining Acquiring's and Target's 5-percent shareholders, and (iii) additional information obtained through the Written Inquiries.
(e) The Proposed Transaction will qualify as a tax-free reorganization within the meaning of § 368(a).
migration_review: Added 2026-06-04 from full-text validation pass. Contains exact phrase: 'the presumption described in § 1.382-2T(j)(2)(iii)(B)(1) will not apply' at Ruling (2).

issuing_office: Office of Associate Chief Counsel (Corporate), CC:CORP:5
signatory: Richard K. Passales, Senior Counsel, Branch 4
uilc_dictionary_mapping: code: 0382.00-00 | title: Limitation on Net Operating Loss Carryforwards and Certain Built-in Losses Following Ownership Changes
key_authorities_cited:
[Segregation Rules]
Treas. Reg. § 1.382-2T(j)(2) - segregation rules upon equity structure shift
Treas. Reg. § 1.382-2T(j)(2)(iii)(B)(1) - presumption of non-overlap between public groups
Treas. Reg. § 1.382-2T(k)(2) - actual knowledge of stock ownership
transaction_type:
Reverse merger / tax-free reorganization under § 368(a) (also equity structure shift described in § 381(a)(2))
Completed (not proposed) transaction; both Acquiring and Target widely-held publicly traded consolidated groups in related industries (Business A and Business B)
issues_presented:
(1) Whether public documents + responses to Written Questionnaires (and follow-up communications) constitute an acceptable method of determining Actual Knowledge under § 1.382-2T(k)(2).
(2) Whether Acquiring may use the Actual Knowledge to treat the Overlapping Public Group as an additional direct public group for purposes of the segregation rules of § 1.382-2T(j)(2), with the presumption in § 1.382-2T(j)(2)(iii)(B)(1) not applying.
(3) How the increase in the Overlapping Public Group's percentage ownership is measured.
conclusions:
(1) YES - SEC public documents + Written Questionnaires + follow-up are an acceptable method of determining Actual Knowledge within Treas. Reg. § 1.382-2T(k)(2).
(2) YES - Acquiring may treat the Overlapping Public Group as an additional direct public group for § 1.382-2T(j)(2); the presumption described in Treas. Reg. § 1.382-2T(j)(2)(iii)(B)(1) will not apply.
(3) The increase = percentage ownership of Acquiring immediately after the Acquisition minus percentage ownership immediately before.
subject_summary: Segregation-rule presumption rebuttal post-completion: Acquiring (with Shareholder X as known 5% holder) used SEC filings (including Form 13Fs) plus Written Questionnaires to identify Overlapping Public Group between two related-industry consolidated groups; IRS rules that the presumption described in Treas. Reg. § 1.382-2T(j)(2)(iii)(B)(1) will not apply, permitting collapse of the overlap into a single direct public group.
timeline_vector: [Pre-Date 1: Acquiring has Shareholder X (5%, b shares); remaining stock owned by Acquiring Public Groups] -> [Pre-Date 1: Target has no 5% shareholders; all stock owned by Target Public Groups] -> [Pre-Date 1: Acquiring forms Merger Sub in anticipation of Acquisition] -> [Date 1: Merger Sub merges with and into Target pursuant to State A law per Merger Agreement; Target survives as wholly-owned subsidiary of Acquiring] -> [Date 1: § 368(a) reorganization completed; also equity structure shift described in § 381(a)(2)] -> [Post-Acquisition: Acquiring shareholders own d% of Acquiring; former Target shareholders own e%] -> [Post-Acquisition: Acquiring identifies Overlapping Public Group via IR inquiries + SEC filings (Form 13Fs) + Written Questionnaires + follow-up correspondence] -> [Owner-shift measurement: Overlapping Public Group treated as single additional direct public group; presumption of Treas. Reg. § 1.382-2T(j)(2)(iii)(B)(1) does not apply]
timeline_vector_raw: [Pre-Date 1: Acquiring pre-transaction snapshot - Shareholder X 5%; Acquiring Public Groups] -> [Pre-Date 1: Target pre-transaction snapshot - no 5% shareholders] -> [Date 1: Merger Sub -> Target merger under State A law; § 368(a) reorg + § 381(a)(2) ESS] -> [Post-Date 1: Acquiring d%, former Target e%] -> [Post-Date 1: SEC filings review + Written Questionnaires sent + follow-up] -> [Owner-shift testing: Overlapping Public Group collapsed to single direct public group]
favorable_logic_pivot: Same logic as PLR 201110006 but applied to a COMPLETED transaction in related industries (where overlap is intuitively likely because shared market-segment funds with industry-investment strategies hold both stocks). Key extension: written 'Questionnaires' + follow-up correspondence in any form (written/digital/telephonic) are sufficient to establish Actual Knowledge under § 1.382-2T(k)(2). The presumption of § 1.382-2T(j)(2)(iii)(B)(1) yields once Actual Knowledge of overlap is established, even though the inquiry methodology relies heavily on SEC public filings rather than direct shareholder confirmation alone. Confirms that the rebuttal-pathway is available both pre-closing (1110006) and post-closing (this PLR).
compelled_representations:
(1) Acquiring is a loss corporation as defined in § 382(k)(1).
(2) Acquiring's only class of outstanding stock during the relevant testing period is its common stock.
(3) Acquiring has no other outstanding interests or obligations treated as stock for purposes of § 382.
(4) Acquiring has no actual knowledge regarding members of the Overlapping Public Group other than knowledge obtained through (a) corporate records of Acquiring and Target, (b) survey of relevant SEC filings to determine whether Acquiring and Target had any 5-percent shareholders, and (c) additional information obtained through the Written Questionnaires.
(5) The Acquisition qualified as a tax-free reorganization within the meaning of § 368(a) that is an equity structure shift also described in § 381(a)(2) and in which Acquiring is a party to the reorganization.
migration_review: Added 2026-06-04 from full-text validation pass. Contains exact phrase: 'the presumption described in Treas. Reg. § 1.382-2T(j)(2)(iii)(B)(1) will not apply' at Ruling (2).
